content.en:
Big L2E Algorithms (Last Two Edges)

[Flip] = U' R' U R' F R F', A: B = A B A'. This sheet does not include cases similar to 5x5 ones or cases not obvious. Generally, solve inner L2E then entire L2E. On 6x6, Eparity, Oparity, Pparity may exist; On 7x7, Eparity may exist. If Eparity exists on 6x6, one could cancel it into Oparity using the method below. Ignore the unsolved edge and continue to 3x3 last F2L or OLL stage and put it on UF. OLL stage: If there are even number of oriented outer wings of LL, do inner Eparity; If odd, do outer Eparity. F2L stage: If there are odd number of oriented outer wings of LL and free slot, do inner Eparity; If even, do outer Eparity. CN cuber should choose the opposite color of one of two colors on this edge as cross color. For example, if the unsolved edge is orange-blue, choose red or green as cross color. White-cross cuber should not leave the white edge unsolved, and should try to choose unsolved yellow edges. Remark: Outer Eparity = Flip outer wings; Inner Eparity = Flip inner wings = Outer Eparity + Pparity.

Inner Eparity

Outer Eparity

Inner Eparity

Outer Eparity

6x6

3r': [Flip]

3L: [Flip]

3R' l: [Flip]

l: [Flip] 3R': [Flip]

(↓r U2 x↓r U2) (r U2' r' U2) (l U2 4r' U2') (r U2 r' U2') r' (Outer Eparity)

3r U2 x (3r U2 3R U2) x' (3L' U2 3L U2) (3R' U2 3R U2) 3r' U2 3r' (Inner Eparity)

7x7

(↑3R2' B2 U2') (3R2' U2' B2) 3R2'

(↓3R2 F2 U2') (3R2 U2' F2) 3R2

(U R U' B) (3R2 F2 U2') (3R2 U2' F2) 3R2

r U2 x r U2 (r U2' r' U2) l U2 (5r' U2' r U2) r' U2' r'

3r U2 x 3r U2 (3r U2' 3r' U2) 3l U2 (4r' U2' 3r U2) 3r' U2' 3r'

x· (3r2' F 3R' F) (U2' F' 3R F U2') (3r U2 3R U2) (3r' F2 3r2)

x· (3r2' F 3r' r) (F U2' F') (3r r' F U2') (3r U2 3r r' U2) (3r' F2 3r2)

↓3r U2 x· ↓(3r U2 3R U2) x'·(3L' U2· 3L U2) (3R' U2 3R U2) (3r' U2 3r')

↓3r U2 x· ↓(3r U2 3r r' U2) x'·(3l' l· U2 3l l' U2) (3r' r U2 3r r' U2) (↑3r' U2 3r')

3R U2 3R U2 x U2 3R U2 x' 3L' U2 3L U2 3R2

3R2' B2 3R' U2 3R' U2 x' U2 3R' U2 3R U2 3R' U2 3R2

3r' U2 3r' U2 x' r' U2 3r' U2 4r U2 3l' r2 U2 3r2 r' U2 r

To do this with twsearch from the command line (assuming you have some sequence that generates this position) we find that this is essentially the *only* such length-18 solution in the moves r,3r,l,3l,U2,F2.

./twsearch --scramblealg "3r' U2 3r' U2 r' B2 3r' B2 3l U2 3l' r2 U2 3r2 r' U2 r U2" -c 20 --moves r,3r,l,3l,U2,F2 symmsamples/7x7x7obtm.tws

...

U2 3l' U2 3l' U2 l' F2 3l' F2 3r U2 3r' l2 U2 3l2 l' U2 l

U2 r U2 r' 3r2 U2 r2 3l' U2 3l F2 3r' F2 r' U2 3r' U2 3r'

l' U2 3l2 l U2 3r l2 U2 3r' F2 3l F2 l U2 3l U2 3l U2

3r U2 3r U2 r F2 3r F2 3l' U2 r2 3l U2 r 3r2 U2 r' U2

Takes nearly a minute.

Tomas Rokicki
